title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z — the online generation — is increasingly struggling with loneliness, and dating app giant Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable social events for young people.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funds target New York, Los Angeles and London.
Goal is to support free or affordable social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge's One More Hour initiative.
Framed as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: No direct implications for grid or data center operations are mentioned in the description.
The initiative is focused on in-person social events rather than new digital infrastructure.
If any indirect effects exist (e.g., event-related digital services), they are not detailed in the description.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent understands your company-specific information. The talk promises an end-to-end build with no graph theory or query expertise required.
bullets: Automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Connects the knowledge graph to an AI agent that understands your business.
No graph theory or graph-query knowledge required.
End-to-end live build demonstrated.
References a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB for generative AI agents.
takeaways: Enables AI agents to work with organization-specific data so they can answer business-focused questions.
Automating KG creation reduces the need for specialized graph skills, lowering training and staffing barriers.
An end-to-end, no-expertise approach can accelerate deployment of knowledge-powered AI in enterprise environments.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights that energy incentives are only part of the tax picture for sustainability projects. She emphasizes less obvious opportunities—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation in packaging or processes—and urges leaders to view sustainability investments through a broader tax lens to unlock value.
bullets: Video features Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner, PwC US.
Energy incentives are common but not the only tax tools for sustainability.
Less obvious options include state/local abatements and location-based incentives.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts (e.g., reimagining packaging or processes).
Key message: broaden your tax view to realize meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
takeaways: Don’t assume only energy credits apply—investigate state and local abatements and location-based incentives when siting or expanding facilities.
Evaluate R&D tax credit eligibility for innovation in operational processes as part of sustainability initiatives.
Incorporate a broader set of tax incentives into project planning so sustainability investments can deliver additional financial value.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build: u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists the full winning system specs (Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO), and credits host Dwight (_MightyPotato) with links to the PCPartPicker list and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build (Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build).
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small-form-factor); GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge (mini‑ITX), Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB TeamGroup DDR5-6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker link provided for the full winning system parts list; host credited as Dwight (_MightyPotato).
takeaways: This build demonstrates a consumer RTX 5090 Founders Edition being integrated into a small-form-factor system—useful as an example when assessing consumer GPUs for compact workstation deployments.
A 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000) is listed, showing compact high‑wattage power solutions can be paired with top‑tier consumer GPUs in tight enclosures.
An AIO liquid cooler (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) is used for thermal management in the SFF chassis—relevant when considering cooling strategies for dense or space‑constrained builds.
The combination of a mini‑ITX B850 motherboard and a Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU illustrates a high‑performance CPU+GPU pairing in a small footprint, which may inform compact workstation design choices.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions, unlocking new revenue streams. Sreeni Alakappan explains that leveraging Google Cloud's infrastructure helped streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations and improve developer productivity.
The engagement enabled TTEC to unlock entirely new revenue streams.
Google Cloud Consulting helped compress the company’s digital transformation timeline.
Partnership expanded the company’s ambitions for what’s possible with AI.
takeaways: Rapid prototyping and cloud-native deployment can translate AI pilots into new revenue opportunities.
Robust cloud infrastructure supports operational efficiency gains and higher developer productivity.
Engaging cloud consulting services can shorten transformation timelines and scale ambitions.
Integrating GenAI into customer experience technology can be a strategic path to business growth.
